methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
TS Conductor is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: KoBold Metals (Series C, $3.0B), Electric Hydrogen (Series C, $1.0B), and Amogy (Series C, $700M). The broader frontier-tech comparables universe includes eight private peers and one public company (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a typical Series B-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.9x to the $60M base, implying $114M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with observed range of $700M to $3.0B. The peer-median method carries material weight given the specificity of the comp set, though the wide dispersion ($700M–$3.0B) reflects heterogeneity in technology readiness and capital efficiency across the cohort.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $97M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $557M. The wide spread reflects tension between stage-progression mechanics and peer-based pricing. Key uncertainties include the timing and path to Series C fundraising, relative technology de-risking versus peers, and the sustainability of Climate & Energy sector valuations in a shifting capital environment.
